bead.title: fix executor: enforce parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness children on Linux
bead.description:
PROBLEM
When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, or supervising-process loss) while an execute-bead attempt is running, the spawned harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27: a `claude --print` execute-bead child remained orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent exited.

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness via `exec.Command(binary, args...)` and then calls `cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)`.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. There is no Linux `Pdeathsig`, so the kernel does not signal the harness when the parent dies abnormally.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 kills the process group only from graceful executor paths.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 uses `signal.NotifyContext` for os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so executor cleanup does not run.

PROPOSED FIX
On Linux, set `SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig` to SIGKILL or SIGTERM for execute-bead harness commands in `cmdSetProcessGroup`. Handle the Go runtime thread-migration caveat with `runtime.LockOSThread` around process start or an equivalent parent-PID guard so the parent-death signal is attached to the intended parent thread. Preserve process-group setup so existing graceful cancellation and timeout cleanup still kill the whole harness tree. Document the macOS/Windows fallback because `Pdeathsig` is Linux-only.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.
- Startup orphan reaping is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.
- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.
- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestExecutorLinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL` starts an execute-bead executor path against a fake harness binary that forks a long-lived grandchild, SIGKILLs the parent worker process rather than sending graceful SIGTERM, and asserts the harness child and grandchild are gone within a bounded grace instead of becoming PID-1 orphans.
2. `TestCmdSetProcessGroupLinuxSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` verifies the Linux executor command configuration includes both process-group isolation and a parent-death signal.
3. `TestExecutorStartParentDeathSignalThreadGuard` or an equivalent focused test/rationale covers the `Pdeathsig` parent-thread caveat using `runtime.LockOSThread` or a parent-PID guard.
4. `TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree` proves existing SIGINT/SIGTERM or context-cancellation cleanup still terminates in-flight harness children.
5. Non-Linux behavior is covered by `TestExecutorParentDeathSignalNonLinuxFallbackDocumented` or build-tagged documentation explaining the fallback and why `Pdeathsig` is unavailable.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
